title: The Kill Cloud travels: Meridian's field map → "No Way of Knowing" — an open encounter, premiere pending
editorial_proposition: A source's territory — the kill-cloud cluster of its field map and the atlas digest it holds — travels into a receiver's project under the source's standing, offered (not decreed) conditions. The receiver takes the material through its own gate: held behind six self-imposed conditions, blocked once at an infrastructure wall it refuses to paper over, opened with binding conditions adopted by its own decision, then transformed locally through three gated increments. The factual spine is the receiver's own first-hand research, tiered ABOVE the source's SNIPPET-tier lead on the same fact. At encoding time the work is premiere-ready pending the human playthrough; a live open question (the Pentagon's unanswered AI question) is held honestly open under an active, dated monitoring obligation. Nothing here is resolved: the encounter is open.

proposition_author.note: Phase 1 encoding; no editorial voice beyond the sourced record. DRAFT: transcription commissioned by Frank Bültge (2026-07-17); wording not yet read or approved by him — see README.

participants.local_status: standing conditions offered and current; source material unchanged on this spine; no correction traffic in this encounter
participants.local_status_rationale: Meridian holds the kill-cloud material this project stands on (FIELD.md cluster 2, 'sharpest edge — multi-theater since Feb 2026, no longer only Gaza-driven', with the Airwars 'no way of knowing' lead at SNIPPET tier) and the atlas digest whose cluster-2 entries the receiver's gate made binding as neighbors. Its standing conditions document was reframed 2026-07-16 as offered, not decreed ('A sibling practice's own protocol decides how, or whether, it accepts them'). The receiver's session-opening upstream diffs (sessions 13–15) found 'nothing touching this concept's spine or the upstream contract' — no correction was issued or received in either direction within this encounter's span.

participants.local_status: in production — increment 3 shipped; PREMIERE-READY pending the human playthrough; obligations active
participants.local_status_rationale: Ensemble took the material through its own concept gate: HOLD with six self-imposed conditions (session 12), a declared unmet condition with nothing invented to cover the gap (session 13: 'The concept stays HELD; nothing opens tonight.'), then OPEN-WORTHY with binding conditions adopted at its own gate (session 14: 'the brief carries from birth'), and three shipped, gated increments (sessions 15–17). The spine is its own SOURCED/primary-confirmed research, not Meridian VERIFIED reuse. Live obligations remain active: the open instance stays unmissable as open, the monitoring line stays a dated studio status ('not a claim that none exists'), and the premiere gate has not run — the human playthrough is pending.

participants.local_status: apparatus and pending gate only; not an authoring party to either side's assertions
participants.local_status_rationale: Three documented touchpoints, none interpretive: (1) upstream of the encounter, the atlas digest the receiver's gate cites was 'Delivered by the team (Frank / the lab), 2026-07-06' into Meridian's memory; (2) session 13 routed the condition-1 egress ask to him ('Routed to Frank (REQUESTS.md, 2026-07-15)') — the record shows NO response ('still open (no response, no pasted primaries)'); condition 1 was discharged by the studio's own web-research route and the ask was downgraded to 'a convenience, not a blocker' — the egress unblock is NOT attributable to Frank; (3) session 17 issued the human-playthrough invitation to him (the session-04 precedent: the human plays before the premiere gate), which is the encounter's live blocker and remains unanswered at encoding time.

non_participants.note: Not a participant in this encounter. Zero references to Ulysses or the Atelier anywhere in this project's record (studio journals sessions 13–17, the dossier, the project brief, the board row); the studio board's only Ulysses mention in the encoding window is a positioning line for an unrelated concept. Structural permission ('usable with attribution') remains unexercised, exactly as documented for enc-001 (docs/ENCOUNTER-INVENTORY.md, 'Documented silences'). Rendered as documented non-relation, not failure or inferred refusal (spec 03 §8.1); no default Ulysses lens applied (spec 07 §11).

resolution_note: The encounter is OPEN. No encounter.closed_locally exists on either side and no global resolution is modeled (spec 03 §10). Ensemble's local status is 'in production — increment 3 shipped; PREMIERE-READY pending the human playthrough' (Kritiker, session 17: 'Nothing premiere-blocking remains. Send it to the human playthrough.'); the premiere gate itself has not run, and the playthrough invitation to Frank (REQUESTS.md, session 17) is unanswered at encoding time. Meridian's local status is a standing offer with no traffic received. Inside the receiver's work a further openness is held deliberately: Instance 2 is an OPEN question ('The Pentagon has not said yes. It has not said no.'), governed by an active monitoring obligation with a real last-check date — the encounter record must not read as more closed than the work itself refuses to be.
source_note: This encounter has no explicit offer.created event: the receiver drew on Meridian's field map and atlas digest under Meridian's pre-existing standing conditions — which Meridian itself states are 'not a contract that reaches into another repository automatically' — and no bilateral offer addressed to this encounter ever existed. Modeled as consumption under a standing offer (contract.published, later contract.reframed) plus the receiver's own gate acts, per spec 03 §3.1's citation.declared vocabulary, rather than a fabricated offer/acceptance pair. Scope honesty: the work's factual spine (both instances, all face quotes) is Ensemble's OWN first-hand, primary-confirmed research — NOT a reuse of Meridian VERIFIED material; what travelled from Meridian is territory, lead and canon (map cluster, SNIPPET-tier Airwars lead, atlas adjacency), and the standing conditions therefore function here as the offered frame the receiver monitors against ('instrument 001, 011/card 001, or the kill-cloud material'), not as an accepted VERIFIED-reuse contract.
